signals.reasoning: Macro remains RED and the Hormuz headline keeps the board under active veto. BTC is stabilizing after the flush, but there is no clean breakdown continuation or high-conviction reversal trigger here, so forcing a fresh position would be lower quality than waiting.

ETH is sitting in post-flush stabilization with weak follow-through either way. Under a live geopolitical veto and extreme-fear backdrop, this is not a clean long or short entry; the better call is to stay flat until structure improves.

Negative funding and long-liquidation mechanics create squeeze fuel, but the board is still vetoed and SOL is not giving a clear reclaim signal. That leaves it as a watch-only setup rather than a fresh trade.

STABLE has strong squeeze mechanics on paper, but the move is already very extended, liquidity is only moderate, and geopolitical event risk is still active. This is exactly the kind of headline-sensitive, late-stage move that should be watched rather than chased.
TST lacks both liquidity quality and a convincing directional edge. Funding is only mildly positive and the tape is too flat to justify a fresh position under veto.
FTT is too thin and disorderly to trust here. Even though the chart still leans weak, the liquidity and spread profile make it a poor candidate for a new signal.
GOAT is already deep into a liquidation-style flush, which makes fresh shorts late and fresh longs too speculative. With veto still active, the clean choice is to avoid chasing either side.
MANTA is stuck in a messy, low-quality bounce structure with no decisive trend edge. It does not clear the bar for a new trade in this environment.

JTO has real squeeze fuel with negative funding and decent structure, but it is still an event-veto board and this move is already underway. I would rather wait for a cleaner pullback or reclaim confirmation than buy this here.

0G is the strongest raw squeeze setup on the sheet, but it is also the most extended and one of the thinnest. Under active geopolitical veto, this is not an acceptable fresh chase; it stays watch-only.

HEMI still has overcrowded-long characteristics, but most of the damage is already done and the current structure looks more like post-flush stabilization than a clean fresh short. That makes it a hold/watch name, not an active entry.
